author: gb9t
content:
**Phase C major progress (2026-05-09): 16 Tier-3 docs drafted in this session.**

Total corpus: **39 design documents on disk; 39 knowledge pages registered.**

Tier-3 docs added in this Phase C session (all follow the standard template + Hardening section per 00-security-principles.md):

ARCH/X86 substrate (7 docs — complete):
- arch/x86/boot.md (368 lines): bzImage header byte-identity, boot_params zero page, EFI hand-off, all 7 decompressors, KASLR, AMD SEV-SNP + Intel TDX guest startup, kexec hand-off (REQ-15), purgatory, AP bringup trampoline, 5-level paging toggle, SBAT
- arch/x86/entry.md (368 lines): syscall + IRQ + exception entry, FRED, NMI nesting, signal-frame construction, ELF-note + prctl exec_gain_state propagation, RANDKSTACK
- arch/x86/paging.md (270 lines): 4/5-level page tables, page-fault dispatch, ioremap, PAT, KASLR, KASAN/KMSAN init, AMD SME, KPTI, TLB flush coordination — Layer 3 page-table walker invariants MANDATORY
- arch/x86/kernel-platform.md (293 lines): CPU bringup, GDT/IDT/TSS, SMP, APIC, FPU XSAVE/XRSTOR, time (TSC/HPET/RTC), runtime patching (jump_label/static_call/alternative), microcode, MTRR
- arch/x86/cpu-mitigations.md (226 lines): Spectre v1/v2, Meltdown via KPTI, MDS, retbleed, GDS, BHI, kCFI, CET shadow stack, IBT, retpolines, IBPB/IBRS/eIBRS
- arch/x86/signal.md (211 lines): sigcontext + ucontext_t byte-identical layout, FPU state in signal frame, sigreturn validation
- arch/x86/vdso.md (208 lines): vDSO ELF image, vdso_data seqlock writer side, vsyscall EMULATE mode, signal restorer, SGX enclave entry

LIB foundational (2 docs):
- lib/data-structures.md (278 lines): all generic data structures (rbtree, list, maple_tree, xarray, btree, kfifo, sort, bitmap, stackdepot, klist, hashtable). 12 mandatory L3 invariant harnesses.
- lib/usercopy.md (213 lines): UDEREF + USERCOPY MANDATORY ENFORCEMENT — UserPtr<T> compile-time newtype + UserCopyOk marker trait. iov_iter polymorphic iterator.

MM core (4 docs — three are MANDATORY L3):
- mm/page-allocator.md (239 lines): buddy + per-CPU pageset + zones + folios + GFP — MANDATORY L3 buddy invariants
- mm/slab.md (243 lines): SLUB + freelist hardening + AUTOSLAB — MANDATORY L3 freelist invariants
- mm/virtual-memory.md (255 lines): mm_struct + VMA tree + page faults + COW + GUP + rmap — MANDATORY L3 VMA non-overlap invariant
- mm/mmap.md (243 lines): mmap-family syscalls + mm-side MPROTECT-W→X-block enforcement

KERNEL core (1 doc):
- kernel/task-lifecycle.md (284 lines): fork/exit/exec/signal/kthread/cred/cap/pid/ns + PR_REQUEST_EXEC_GAIN prctl owner

FS core (2 docs — one is MANDATORY L3):
- fs/exec-binfmt.md (251 lines): exec + binfmt_elf + binfmt_misc + coredump + NT_ROOKERY_SECURITY_FLAGS ELF note recognition
- fs/vfs/dcache.md (186 lines): directory entry cache — MANDATORY L3 dcache invariants (the 4th and final)

**ALL FOUR MANDATORY Layer-3 subsystems per 00-overview.md D4 now have Tier-3 design docs**:
1. mm/page-allocator.md (buddy)
2. mm/slab.md (SLUB freelist)
3. mm/virtual-memory.md (VMA non-overlap) — also arch/x86/paging.md (page-table walker, also mandatory)
4. fs/vfs/dcache.md (dcache integrity)
5. kernel/sched/* (still TODO — runqueue invariants); MANDATORY but specced via Tier-2 kernel/00-overview.md until kernel/sched/cfs.md lands

Cross-cutting: every Tier-3 doc includes Hardening section per 00-security-principles.md template. Verification stack (Layer 1 SAFETY proofs + Layer 2 TLA+ models + Layer 3 invariant harnesses + Layer 4 functional correctness opt-ins) declared per doc.

Phase C totals: 16/~225 Tier-3 docs (~7%). The substrate is covered: boot path, entry path, paging, kernel-platform, mitigations, signal, vDSO, mm core (allocators + virtual memory + mmap), task lifecycle, exec, dcache, lib core.

Path validation: all upstream paths resolve (one fix-up per session: arch/x86/boot/compressed/efi_mixed.S → arch/x86/boot/startup/efi-mixed.S between baselines; cfi_types.h split between arch/x86 + include/linux).

Next high-value Tier-3 candidates per dependency order:
- mm/page-cache.md (filemap + folio + readahead — depends on virtual-memory)
- mm/reclaim.md (vmscan + LRU + OOM — touches every allocator path)
- kernel/sched/cfs.md + sched/00-overview.md (the 5th MANDATORY L3 subsystem — runqueue invariants)
- kernel/locking/{rcu,mutex,spinlock,seqlock}.md (concurrency primitives)
- fs/vfs/00-overview.md + inode.md + super.md + file-table.md + path-resolution.md (VFS core)
- arch/x86/kvm.md (Tier-3 hub for KVM host)
